summary: Frontend-only regression testing completed for Docs-like redesign. Verified header/toolbar load, comments rail open/close, history rail open, collaboration preview inline remote typing label/caret, share button visibility, page switching, and published page navigation. No functional regressions were observed in the requested flows.

design_issues.screen: Editor with version history rail open
issues: History rail metadata/body text appears very low-contrast against white background, reducing readability/accessibility.

action_items: Increase contrast for history rail secondary text to improve readability/accessibility while preserving Docs-like visual style.
critical_code_review_comments: EditorWorkspace correctly uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL for API base URL and preserves /api prefix routing.

Requested interaction elements include stable data-testid coverage for primary flows (header, toolbar, panel toggles, comments rail, version history, collaboration preview), which improves regression testability.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on public URL https://realtime-editor.stage-preview.emergentagent.com. Collaboration preview inline label/caret appeared successfully after enabling demo and waiting ~3.6s. Comments and history rails open from toolbar buttons, and page switching + published navigation are functioning.
rca of the issue: Low contrast appears due to light gray typography styling in the history rail against a white surface in the current theme; this is a visual accessibility/readability issue rather than a functional defect.
